reasoning: Duke's AdjEM (37.3) towers over TCU's (15.2) — a 22.1-point gap. Even with Foster out, Duke's generational offense and elite defense overwhelm Jamie Dixon's tournament pedigree. The talent gap is too wide for coaching alone to bridge.

St. John's AdjEM (25.7) exceeds Kansas's (23.5). Pitino's 55-22 tournament record rivals Self's 57-24. Kansas weakened by Peterson's absence and 5-5 L10. St. John's is 9-1 L10 as Big East champs with elite defense (AdjD 94.1). The hot team with marginally better numbers advances.

Izzo's 59-26 tournament record vs Kelsey's 0-6 is the decisive coaching mismatch. Michigan State's AdjEM edge (26.7 vs 25.9) is slim, but Izzo's between-round adjustments and Louisville's Mikel Brown Jr. back injury tip the scales.

UConn's AdjEM (28.1) is 13.8 points above UCF's (14.3). UCF's R64 upset of depleted UCLA won't carry them past two-time defending champions. Hurley's 15-5 tournament record and championship DNA dominate.

Florida's AdjEM (33.8) towers over Iowa's (21.9) — 11.9-point gap. Florida's defense (AdjD 92.3) suffocates Iowa's offense. Todd Golden's 6-2 tournament record and SEC 1-seed pedigree make this comfortable.

Vanderbilt's AdjEM (28.1) edges Nebraska's (23.8) by 4.3 points, driven by a massive offensive gap (AdjO 127.4 vs 117.3). Nebraska's elite defense is formidable, but Vanderbilt's SEC explosiveness and Mast's illness uncertainty tip the scales.

Illinois' AdjEM (33.7) is 17.5 points above VCU's (16.2). VCU's HAVOC defense and upset momentum are real, but Illinois has the tournament's second-best offense (AdjO 131.9). The efficiency gap is too wide for a mid-major to overcome two rounds in a row.

Houston's AdjEM (33.0) is 11.2 points above Saint Mary's (21.8). Both play slow, grinding basketball, but Houston's defense (AdjD 92.4) is dramatically better. Sampson's 31-21 tournament record vs Bennett's 7-11 gives Houston the coaching edge.

Arizona's AdjEM (35.5) is 14.6 points above Utah State's (20.9). The Wildcats' elite defense (AdjD 91.4) will contain Utah State's strong offense. Big 12 champion on a 9-1 run dominates the upset-minded Aggies.

Arkansas's AdjEM (26.3) edges Wisconsin's (25.5). Calipari's 57-22 tournament record (6 FFs, 1 title) is the massive coaching edge vs Gard's 7-7. SEC championship momentum (8-2 L10 HOT) and explosive offense (AdjO 127.9) at a fast tempo overwhelm Wisconsin.

Gonzaga's AdjEM (26.3) holds a 5.9-point edge over BYU's (20.4) with better defense (AdjD 94.0 vs 104.3). Mark Few's 44-25 tournament record and WCC rivalry familiarity give Gonzaga the schematic edge. Even without Huff, the 3-6 gap holds.

Purdue's AdjEM (33.0) is 13.4 points above Miami's (19.6). Nation-best offense (AdjO 133.3) exploits Miami's average defense. Painter's 22-16 tournament record and Big Ten championship momentum dominate first-year tournament coach.

Michigan's AdjEM (36.6) is 19.3 points above Saint Louis's (17.3). Elite defense (AdjD 91.0) smothers Saint Louis's offense. Saint Louis's 28-5 record earned this spot, but the talent gap against a 1-seed is too vast.

Alabama's AdjEM (26.7) is comparable to Texas Tech's (27.5), but Alabama's explosive offense at blazing tempo (73.1) creates a track meet. Oats' 11-6 tournament record and SEC experience edge McCasland's 3-3 Big 12 record.

Virginia's AdjEM (26.4) edges Tennessee's (26.0) — razor-thin. Both elite defensive teams at deliberate tempos. Virginia's 8-2 L10 HOT momentum as ACC runner-up vs Tennessee's 6-4 neutral is the tiebreaker.

Iowa State's AdjEM (31.1) is 9.8 points above Santa Clara's (21.3). Elite defense (AdjD 92.6) disrupts Santa Clara's offense. Otzelberger's 9-5 tournament record and Big 12 pedigree overwhelm WCC roster.
